microsoftSourceClaims.claimText: Microsoft Graph exposes Microsoft 365 service health through the serviceAnnouncement healthOverviews resource, readable with the ServiceHealth.Read.All permission (delegated work or school, and application).
Microsoft 365 service health is viewed on the Service health page in the Microsoft 365 admin center and is distinct from Azure Service Health, which reports on Azure resources.
Microsoft Sentinel is generally available in the Microsoft Defender portal for unified security operations; the standalone Azure portal experience is scheduled for retirement on 31 March 2027.

The Microsoft 365 (Office 365) Sentinel data connector ingests Exchange, SharePoint, and Teams audit logs; there is no native connector for Microsoft 365 service health or Copilot interaction events, which are available through the Office 365 Management API.

The legacy Microsoft Graph security alerts API at /security/alerts is deprecated and is scheduled for retirement on 31 August 2026; implementations should use /security/alerts_v2 with SecurityAlert.Read.All.

licenses: Microsoft 365 E3 or E5 test tenant with Copilot-enabled workloads
Microsoft 365 Copilot license assigned to the lab operator where Copilot dependency checks are exercised
roles: Global Reader or Service Support Administrator for read-only Microsoft 365 service health access
Security Reader for read-only inspection of Microsoft Defender portal and any existing Microsoft Sentinel workspace state
features: Microsoft 365 service health page available in the Microsoft 365 admin center
Microsoft Graph serviceAnnouncement read access preauthorized with ServiceHealth.Read.All when the optional Graph query is exercised
capacityRequirements: One disposable test workspace directory for representative sample evidence output
dataRequirements: Representative sample service-health records only; no production incident narratives
prerequisites.notes: First lab cycle is read-only and detect-only. No connectors, analytics rules, workbooks, Logic Apps, diagnostic settings, support tickets, role assignments, or retention changes are created. No raw tenant identifiers, user identities, support-ticket content, full incident narratives, tokens, or secrets are retained.

phases.title: Confirm scope, identity, and least-privilege access

steps.intent: Positively prove the target tenant identity read-only by viewing the organization profile, and record only a non-identifying attestation (for example, an initialed confirmation) rather than the raw tenant identifier.
steps.expected: The organization profile confirms the expected tenant without the operator recording the raw tenant ID, domain, or directory GUID.
steps.locator: Microsoft 365 admin center > Settings > Org settings > Organization profile

readBack.expectation: Operator attests the correct tenant is in scope without capturing raw identifiers.

steps.intent: Confirm the lab operator holds only read-only roles (Global Reader or Service Support Administrator) needed for service-health inspection.
steps.expected: Assigned roles are read-only; no elevation is performed. Missing roles are captured as an evidence-backed BLOCKED path.
steps.locator: Microsoft Entra admin center > Roles and administrators

readBack.expectation: Read-only role membership is confirmed or a missing-role BLOCKED disposition is recorded with source evidence.

phases.title: Inspect service health and run representative sample scripts

steps.intent: Inspect the Microsoft 365 admin center Service health page read-only to observe current advisories and incidents for Copilot-dependent workloads.
steps.expected: Service health status is observed. A no-current-incident state is captured as evidence rather than treated as a failure.
steps.locator: Microsoft 365 admin center > Health > Service health

readBack.expectation: Current workload health is recorded, including an explicit no-current-incident note when applicable.

steps.intent: Optionally query Microsoft Graph service health read-only with least-privilege access, only when preauthorized with ServiceHealth.Read.All.
steps.expected: Health overviews are returned read-only, or a missing-permission BLOCKED path is recorded when consent is not present.

readBack.expectation: Response is read-only and no raw tenant identifiers are retained, or a missing-permission BLOCKED disposition is recorded.

steps.intent: Run the representative Monitor-Compliance script against the local stub or sample payload to produce a documentation-first monitoring snapshot.
steps.expected: The snapshot includes runtime-mode markers, DORA severity classification, and a freshness summary; overall status is not reported as implemented for stub or sample input.

readBack.expectation: Snapshot contains runtimeMode, DataSourceMode, and Freshness fields with an honest non-implemented status.

steps.intent: Run the representative Export-Evidence script to produce service-health-log, incident-register, and resilience-test-results artifacts with SHA-256 companion files.
steps.expected: Three artifacts and matching .sha256 files are written, each carrying collection time, source last-modified time, and freshness metadata.

readBack.expectation: Artifacts and hash files exist and the package validates against data/evidence-schema.json.

steps.intent: Inspect any existing Microsoft Sentinel or Microsoft Defender portal integration state read-only, only where a workspace already exists. Do not onboard Sentinel or enable connectors, content, or rules.
steps.expected: Existing workspace or connector state is observed read-only, or a no-Sentinel-workspace NOT-APPLICABLE path is recorded because this solution requires customer-built ingestion.
steps.locator: Microsoft Defender portal > System > Settings > Microsoft Sentinel

readBack.expectation: Read-only observation is recorded, or a no-workspace/no-connector NOT-APPLICABLE disposition is captured with source evidence.

phases.title: Verify freshness, evidence integrity, and source currency

steps.intent: Verify that exported artifacts distinguish collection time from source last-modified time, evaluate staleness, and surface missing timestamps as explicit gaps rather than appearing current.
steps.expected: Freshness objects report status of current, stale, gap, or not-applicable with UTC ISO 8601 timestamps; stale or gap states surface a warning.

readBack.expectation: Freshness distinguishes collection time and source last-modified time and flags gaps or staleness explicitly.

steps.intent: Verify the already-generated evidence package and artifact files without regenerating output by validating package schema, artifact existence, and SHA-256 sidecars read-only.
steps.expected: Validation exits nonzero for missing artifacts, missing sidecars, hash mismatches, or invalid package schema; no evidence files are regenerated.

readBack.expectation: Hash validation passes for the evidence package and all artifacts.

steps.intent: Manually verify read-only source currency for every microsoftSourceClaims URL and the official DORA timeline sources used by evidence and reporting guidance.
steps.expected: All cited sources are rechecked for scope and lifecycle accuracy, including the Sentinel retirement date of 31 March 2027; any stale or conflicting claim is recorded as BLOCKED with follow-up source-citation evidence.
steps.operatorNote: Review each URL read-only and capture disposition evidence: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/serviceannouncement-list-healthoverviews ; https://learn.microsoft.com/microsoft-365/enterprise/view-service-health ; https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/microsoft-sentinel-defender-portal ; https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/connect-office-365 ; https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/alertsv1-alertsv2-migration ; https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj ; https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2025/301/oj ; https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_impl/2025/302/oj ; https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2024/1772/oj . Explicitly flag Sentinel retirement-date drift as BLOCKED and include follow-up evidence references. No network writes are permitted.

readBack.expectation: Each cited URL is reviewed read-only and mapped to a PASS, BLOCKED, or NOT-APPLICABLE note with source evidence and follow-up actions for stale claims.

steps.intent: Capture operator attestation that the first cycle was read-only and detect-only, with no tenant mutations and no retention of sensitive identifiers.
steps.expected: Attestation confirms no connectors, content-hub solutions, workbooks, analytics rules, Logic Apps, diagnostic settings, or Sentinel onboarding were created, and no support tickets, incident mutations, alert closures, role assignments, or retention changes were made.
steps.operatorNote: Confirm no raw tenant identifiers, user identities, support-ticket content, full incident narratives, tokens, or secrets were retained. Only representative sample evidence and non-identifying attestations are kept.

readBack.expectation: Attestation confirms zero tenant mutations and no retention of sensitive identifiers.

requiredArtifacts.description: Read-only capture of the Microsoft 365 admin center Service health page, including any no-current-incident state.
Exported DRM evidence package and artifacts with SHA-256 companion files and freshness metadata.
Transcript showing collection time, source last-modified time, staleness evaluation, and explicit gap handling.
Microsoft Learn source citation confirming lifecycle state and scope for a BLOCKED or NOT-APPLICABLE disposition.
Operator attestation of read-only, detect-only completion with no tenant mutation and no retention of sensitive identifiers.
